concept.title: Data Sync Service API overview
concept.shortdesc: The Data Sync Service API helps you define and run resilient sync jobs to move data from sources to targets (for example, operational systems to a data warehouse) with observability, retries, and idempotency.

ul.li: Platform engineers integrating the service into internal tooling.
Data engineers creating and operating sync jobs.
Site reliability engineers monitoring job runs and incidents.

section.title: Base URL and versioning
section.p: Versioning uses a URI prefix (/v1). Breaking changes are introduced only in new major versions.

ul.li: Idempotent writes: create and update operations support Idempotency-Key. See Authentication.
Async runs: job execution is asynchronous; poll run status or subscribe to webhooks (optional).
Operational visibility: runs include structured error details and timing metrics.
Rate limits: consistent limits per API key; clients should implement backoff on HTTP 429. See Error handling.

ol.li: Create a connector for a source system.
Create a connector for a target (warehouse).
Create a sync job referencing both connectors.
Run the job and monitor the run status.
Backfill or replay runs when needed.
